title: Histone Extraction HLB Protocol
author: Sigrid Verhelst
materials: Start from a dry (snap-frozen) cell pellet

steps.title: Add hypotonic lysis buffer (HLB) to cell pellet
steps.instruction: Add hypotonic lysis buffer (HLB) to cell pellet: 200 µL for 1x10 6 cells and resuspend softly HLB buffer: 10 mM Tris-HCl pH 8.0, 1 mM KCl, 1.5 mM MgCl2 supplemented with 1 mM DTT, 1 mM PMSF, Halt Protease and Phosphatase Inhibitor Cocktail 100x (78440) and phosphatase inhibitor cocktails II and III (P5726 and P0044, Sigma-Aldrich, 1 mL of cocktail for 100 mL of buffer)

steps.title: Rotate for 30 min at 4°C...
steps.instruction: Rotate for 30 min at 4°C to promote lysis of cell membrane (mechanical shear)

steps.title: Pellet the nuclei by centrifugation at 10
steps.instruction: Pellet the nuclei by centrifugation at 10.000g for 10 min at 4°C

steps.title: Resuspend the pellet in 0
steps.instruction: Resuspend the pellet in 0.4N hydochloric acid (HCl) by soft pipetting until no clumps left in solution by adding 125 µL HCl for 1x10 6 cells (if necessary: vortex)

steps.title: Incubate for 30 min in acid...
steps.instruction: Incubate for 30 min in acid on rotator at 4°C to promote lysis of nuclei and solubilization of histones

steps.title: Spin down for 10 min at 4°C and 16
steps.instruction: Spin down for 10 min at 4°C and 16.000 g

steps.title: Transfer supernatant to new Eppendorf (histones...
steps.instruction: Transfer supernatant to new Eppendorf (histones are present in the acid since they are alkaline proteins)

steps.title: Add, drop by drop, trichloroacetic acid...
steps.instruction: Add, drop by drop, trichloroacetic acid (TCA) until a final concentration of 33% is reached to promote precipitation of histones and invert the tube several times (results in a milky solution)

steps.title: Incubate on ice for 30min
steps.instruction: Incubate on ice for 30min

steps.title: Spin for 10 min at 4°C and 16
steps.instruction: Spin for 10 min at 4°C and 16.000 g to pellet the histones

steps.title: Remove the supernatant Be careful
steps.instruction: Remove the supernatant Be careful: the pellet is not always visible

steps.title: Add ice-cold acetone (do not resuspend...
steps.instruction: Add ice-cold acetone (do not resuspend the pellet) to remove TCA, make sure the pellet is fully covered with acetone

steps.title: Spin for 5 min at 4°C and 16000 g
steps.instruction: Spin for 5 min at 4°C and 16000 g

steps.title: Remove the supernatant
steps.instruction: Remove the supernatant

steps.title: Add cold acetone again (do not...
steps.instruction: Add cold acetone again (do not resuspend the pellet) to remove TCA

steps.title: Spin for 5 min at 4°C and 16000 g
steps.instruction: Spin for 5 min at 4°C and 16000 g

steps.title: Remove the supernatant
steps.instruction: Remove the supernatant

steps.title: Resuspend in MilliQ water (50 µl for 1x10 6 cells)
steps.instruction: Resuspend in MilliQ water (50 µl for 1x10 6 cells)

steps.title: Transfer 400
steps.instruction: Transfer 400.000 cells to a new Eppendorf tube for gel-electrophoresis (optionally) If there are still clumps left: Spin for 10 min at 4°C and 16000 g and transfer the supernatant in a fresh Eppendorf

steps.title: Vacuum dry the samples (SpeedVac) Store...
steps.instruction: Vacuum dry the samples (SpeedVac) Store both Eppendorf tubes (for propionylation/digestion and SDS-PAGE) at -20°C or -80°C until further use

steps.title: Dry at room temperature for 30...
steps.instruction: Dry at room temperature for 30 min (until no acetone left) Samples can be stored at -80°C or -20°C until further use (propionylation/digestion) or part of the sample can be prepared to perform sodium dodecyl sulfate polyacrylamide gel electrophoresis (SDS-PAGE)
